meta.title: Mercedes‑Benz SLC R172 Engines — The Complete UK Guide

hero.h1: Mercedes‑Benz SLC R172 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the R172 roadster — from the 1.6‑litre M274 to the 3.0‑litre V6 M276 in the SLC 43 AMG. Real UK replacement costs, honest reliability data, and the two‑seat roadster ownership realities that define this generation. Backed by real UK R172 enquiries in 2025 [MB‑VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: Mercedes‑Benz SLC R172 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the R172 SLC specifically — spanning the facelift SLC era (2016–2020) with four‑cylinder and V6 options.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: OM651: 90% confidence — well‑documented diesel four with timing chain and injector seal issues. M274: 85% confidence — proven turbo four with timing chain stretch as the main watchpoint. M276: 85% confidence — robust V6 bi‑turbo with good reliability. All ratings are PROVISIONAL — pending dedicated Engine Pages for each family.

overview.intro: The R172 SLC is the facelifted third‑generation compact roadster from Mercedes‑Benz, produced from 2016 until 2020. Originally launched in 2011 as the SLK‑Class, the R172 received a significant 2016 facelift that brought the SLC name — aligning it with the C‑Class with which it shared technology — along with updated styling, a revised engine line‑up, and the 9G‑Tronic transmission. The SLC range was powered by a selection of turbocharged four‑cylinder petrol and diesel engines, topped by the V6‑powered SLC 43 AMG.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: Mercedes‑Benz R172 • Predecessor: R171 • Successor: None (discontinued) • Sibling body styles in this generation: None (standalone roadster).
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The SLK/SLC range (R172 combined) generated real UK engine enquiries in 2025 [MB‑VERIFIED], with the OM651 diesel and M276 V6 commanding the strongest specialist interest.

items.quote: The 3.0‑litre V6 bi‑turbo is the engine the SLC deserved — 367hp, 520Nm, and a soundtrack that transforms the compact roadster into a genuine AMG. It's the most rewarding engine in the range, with proven reliability across the Mercedes‑Benz line‑up.
items.whoItsFor: enthusiasts who want genuine AMG performance in a compact package.

items.quote: The 2.1‑litre turbo diesel delivers 204hp and 500Nm — ample torque for the lightweight R172 chassis. It's the most economical engine in the range, with a proven track record across the Mercedes line‑up. Timing chain and injector seals are the main watchpoints.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want roadster fun with diesel running costs.

items.quote: The entry‑level 1.6‑litre turbo is the least powerful engine in the range — 156hp in a roadster that deserves more. Timing chain stretch is a documented issue on higher‑mileage examples, and it lacks the character of the larger engines.
items.whoItsFor: buyers on a tight budget who prioritise running costs over performance.

items.quote: The V6 bi‑turbo is the most complex engine in the SLC range — cooling system and turbocharger issues are documented on higher‑mileage examples, and the tight engine bay makes repairs labour‑intensive. A full recon is a four‑figure bill.
items.whoItsFor: AMG buyers with a maintenance budget to match the performance.

items.quote: The 2.0‑litre turbo delivers 184hp and 300Nm — plenty of performance for the lightweight R172 chassis. It's the sweet spot in the range: more power than the SLC 180, lower running costs than the SLC 300 or V6, and the same proven M274 architecture.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want genuine roadster performance without the V6's running costs.

items.quote: The SLC 300 is often overlooked in favour of the SLC 43 AMG — but 245hp from the 2.0‑litre turbo is more than enough to exploit the R172's chassis, and it avoids the V6's higher running costs. It's the performance bargain of the range.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want near‑AMG performance without the AMG maintenance premium.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Entry Petrol — budget for chain inspection
Performance Bargain — more power, same engine
AMG Performance — most powerful, highest costs

economicsRule.title: The R172 rule
economicsRule.text: A £2,800–£4,800 OM651 recon against a £10,000–£18,000 R172 sits at 16–48% of the car's value — well inside the 70% Mercedes‑Benz threshold. Against a £8,000 high‑mileage example, the same recon reaches 35–60% — still viable, but a significant proportion of the car's residual value. A £4,500–£7,500 M276 recon against a £12,000 SLC 43 is 38–63% — on a high‑mileage example, that's borderline. Value the car and its service history first.

problems.title: M274 Timing Chain Stretch
problems.rootCause: The 2.0‑litre turbo's timing chain can stretch over time, particularly on engines with extended service intervals — a rattle on cold start is the classic early warning.
problems.recommendation: At the first sign of cold‑start rattle, get the chain inspected immediately — an £800–£1,500 preventative repair is far cheaper than the £2,500–£4,000 replacement that follows if ignored.

cta.label: Compare M274 engine prices for your SLC →

problems.title: OM651 Timing Chain & Injector Seal Failure
problems.rootCause: The OM651 diesel four‑cylinder is known for two issues: timing chain tensioner wear (shared with the OM651 across the Mercedes range) and injector seal failure ("Black Death") — both are well‑documented and expensive to address if ignored.
problems.recommendation: A £500–£1,200 injector seal or chain inspection is dramatically cheaper than the £2,800–£4,800 replacement that follows if ignored — address it proactively.

cta.label: Compare OM651 engine prices for your SLC →

problems.title: M276 V6 Cooling System & Turbo Issues
problems.rootCause: The 3.0‑litre V6 bi‑turbo's cooling system can develop leaks, and turbocharger seals can fail — the tight engine bay makes repairs labour‑intensive.
problems.recommendation: On a £12,000–£20,000 SLC 43 AMG, a £800–£1,800 cooling system service is cheap insurance — it protects the £5,000–£8,000 engine that sits at the heart of the car.

cta.label: Compare M276 engine prices for your SLC 43 AMG →
